title: Jalgaon readies hospitals for heatwave in Maharashtra - The News Mill
full_text:
Jalgaon District Collector Rohan Ghuge has confirmed that the district administration has made special arrangements in government hospitals in response to a heatwave warning issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) for Maharashtra.
These measures include reserving separate rooms and beds specifically for patients suffering from heat-related illnesses. “The administration has already issued instructions to all doctors, from the Civil Hospital to the Public Health Centre. Separate rooms and beds have been reserved for those suffering from sun-related illnesses, and all medicines have been stocked,” Ghuge stated.
He urged citizens to take precautions during extreme heat conditions, advising them to avoid spending time outdoors during peak afternoon hours. “My appeal to the citizens is that you should not go out during the afternoon. If you must go out, protect yourself by covering your head and carrying a water bottle,” he added.
Ghuge also noted that the health department has circulated detailed advisories outlining preventive measures and warning signs of heat-related illnesses. “You should follow the dos and don’ts we’ve issued regarding what to do and what not to do in such situations. If anyone experiences any discomfort due to sun exposure, they should be aware of the symptoms,” he said.
“All government hospitals have comprehensive treatment facilities for these conditions,” the Collector confirmed.
On Thursday, the IMD issued a warning of an intense heatwave across several regions of Maharashtra, prompting the State Emergency Operations Centre to advise citizens to take necessary precautions.
Temperatures are expected to rise significantly in multiple parts of the state during the forecast period. Hot and humid weather conditions are anticipated in central Maharashtra from April 15 to 18, in Marathwada from April 16 to 18, in Vidarbha from April 15 to 19, and in Konkan from April 15 to 17 at isolated locations.
Authorities have appealed to residents to adhere to safety guidelines throughout the heatwave.
Earlier on Wednesday, the IMD morning bulletin forecast changing weather across various parts of India. It issued a hot and humid weather warning for several regions, including Telangana, Rayalaseema, Madhya Maharashtra, North Interior Karnataka, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Rajasthan, and Jharkhand, predicting heatwave conditions in isolated pockets between April 15 and 20, with varied peak periods.
Meanwhile, in Northeast India, the IMD predicted light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds over Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura between April 15 and 18, with some areas likely to experience heavy rainfall.
In Northwest India, rainfall and snowfall are expected over Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana, accompanied by thunderstorms, strong winds, and possible hailstorms in parts of Jammu and Kashmir.
East India anticipates scattered rainfall with thunderstorms over West Bengal, Sikkim, and Odisha at various intervals from April 15 to 20.
South India is forecasted to experience light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and gusty winds over Interior and Coastal Karnataka between April 15 and 18.
The IMD has advised citizens across all affected regions to remain alert and to take the necessary precautions as weather conditions continue to vary widely throughout the country.
